title: MSEs should be key focus in Export Promotion Mission: Piyush Goyal
content:
Micro and small enterprises (MSEs) and agricultural exports should remain a key focus across all components of the Export Promotion Mission (EPM), Indian Minister of Commerce and Industry Piyush Goyal has said. 
Market access support should be extended beyond Export Promotion Councils to include other field-level organisations engaged in promoting exports, he said. 
Ten EPM components have been operational.

title: ICE cotton eases on stronger US dollar, profit booking
content:
ICE cotton futures eased as a stronger US dollar reduced export competitiveness, while profit-taking followed recent multi-month highs after a sustained rally.
Planting progress remained steady, adding mild pressure to prices in the near term.
However, dry weather in Texas, geopolitical tensions, and strength in crude oil and grains provided underlying support, limiting downside risks.

title: US industrial textile imports fall, consolidate within North America
content:
US industrial textile imports fell nearly 15 per cent YoY in January-February 2026, but trade increasingly consolidated within North America.
Mexico and Canada gained share, while China and India declined.
Exports also remained region-focused, with over 60 per cent going to Mexico and Canada, highlighting sustained nearshoring despite softer demand.

title: Middle East conflict hits UK exports, down 20%: BCC
content:
UK exports to Middle East markets fell 20 per cent in March 2026, far outpacing the 4 per cent dip in non-Arab trade, signalling a clear region-specific disruption. 
Overall export certificates dropped 10 per cent YoY, reflecting delays, rerouting and shipment losses. 
Rising freight, insurance costs and longer lead times are straining SMEs.

title: US’ Rocky Brands Q1 sales up 9.1%; profit hit by tariffs
content:
Rocky Brands has reported net sales up 9.1 per cent to $124.4 million in Q1 2026, with retail rising 16.5 per cent. 
However, $7.1 million tariff costs reduced gross margin to 36.5 per cent and hit profits, with net income down 74.5 per cent. 
Adjusted EPS fell to $0.24. 
The company expects easing tariff pressure and improved margins in the second half of 2026.

title: Canada's Lululemon appoints Esi Eggleston Bracey to board of directors
content:
Lululemon athletica inc. announced Esi Eggleston Bracey, former chief growth & marketing officer at Unilever, has joined its board of directors effective immediately.  
She will stand for election at the 2026 Annual Meeting, replacing Shane Grant, who will not seek re-election, as the company continues refreshing its board and leadership for future growth.

title: Samsung Electronics Announces First Quarter 2026 Results
content: Samsung Electronics today reported financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026. The Company posted KRW 133.9 trillion in consolidated revenue, an all-time quarterly high, representing a quarter-on-quarter (QoQ) increase of 43%. Operating profit was also an all-time high, increasing to KRW 57.2 trillion. The Device Solutions (DS) Division posted a QoQ sales […]
